title: rosa rubiginosa subvar. pallida
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subvar. pallida, also known as the wild dog rose, is a subspecies of the fragrant wild rose plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subvar. pallida include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Rosa rubiginosa subvar. pallida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
